result.summary: Anchore exposes a self-hosted Enterprise REST API, a Reports GraphQL API, and the anchorectl CLI; all documented auth paths use either HTTP basic credentials or an Anchore-issued API key sent via basic auth, and no MCP server was found.

anchore_basic.label: Anchore Enterprise username and password
anchore_basic.setup: Use an Anchore Enterprise user account provisioned in your deployment. Anchore documents HTTP basic auth as a supported authentication method for APIs and `anchorectl`; see [User Authentication](https://docs.anchore.com/current/docs/configuration/user_authentication/) and [Configuring AnchoreCTL](https://docs.anchore.com/current/docs/configuration/anchorectl/). Account creation is deployment-specific (local users, LDAP, or SSO-backed identities), so mint or obtain credentials from your Anchore Enterprise administrator.

anchore_api_key.label: Anchore Enterprise API key
anchore_api_key.setup: In Anchore Enterprise, create an API key from the user-authentication settings described in [API Keys](https://docs.anchore.com/current/docs/configuration/user_authentication/api_keys/). Anchore notes that API keys authenticate over HTTP basic auth with username `_api_key`; see [Configuring AnchoreCTL](https://docs.anchore.com/current/docs/configuration/anchorectl/).
